title: Abbreviations of Vaccines and Other Immunizing Agents | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:
ine (Pneumovax 23) (f ormerly called PPV or
PPV23) 
PRV 
Pentavalent rotavirus vaccine (RotaTeq) (replaced by the term ROTA, then by RV5) 
RIV3 
Recombinant influenza vaccine, trivalent (Flublok)
RIV4 
Recombinant influenza vaccine, quadrivalent (Flublok Quadrivalent)
ROTA 
Rotavirus vaccine (replaced by the terms RV1 and RV5) 
RRV-TV 
Live, tetravalent rotavirus vaccine (RotaShield) (no longer available) 
RSV 
Respiratory syncytial virus vaccine (Abrysvo, Arexvy,
mResvia)
RSV-mAb 
Respiratory syncytial virus monoclonal antibody (Beyfortus/nirsevimab, Enflonsia/clesrovimab)
RV1 
Rotavirus vaccine, monovalent (Rotarix) (formerly called ROTA) 
RV5 
Rotavirus vaccine, pentavalent (RotaTeq) (formerly called ROTA) 
rVSV Δ G-ZEBOV-GP Ebola 
Recombinant vesicular
stomatitis virus–Zaire Ebola virus vaccine (or Ebola Zaire vaccine, Ervebo)
RZV 
Zoster vaccine, recombinant
(Shingrix)
Top of Page 
S – Z 
TBE 
Inactivated, tick-borne encephalitis
vaccine (Ixiaro)
Td 
Tetanus & diphtheria vaccine (Tenivac, Tdvax) 
Tdap 
Tetanus, diphtheria, and acellular pertussis vaccine (Adacel, Boostrix)
TIV 
Trivalent inactivated influenza vaccine (replaced by the term
IIV) 
TT 
Tetanus toxoid (no longer available) 
Ty21a 
Live oral typhoid vaccine (Vivotif)
VAR 
Varicella vaccine (Varivax)
ViCPS 
Vi capsular polysaccharide inactivated typhoid vaccine (Typhim
Vi)
YF 
Yellow fever vacci
